title: The International Call of Abraham
summary: “Neither shall thy name any more be called Abram, but thy name shall be Abraham; for a father of many nations have I made thee.” (Genesis 17:5). “Seeing that Abraham shall surely become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall be blessed in him?” (Genesis 18:18). We mistakenly think that Abraham was a Jew and the father of Israel alone. It comes as a surprise to some that Abraham was a Syrian. “And thou shalt speak and say before the LORD thy God, A Syrian ready to perish was my father, and he went down into Egypt, and sojourned there with a few, and became there a nation, great, mighty, and populous.”(Deuteronomy 26:5). Abraham was a Syrian who abandoned his country and ethnic roots because God offered him an infinitely grander vision – to become the father of many nations. In this respect, Abraham was unique – he was the first to do this. Had he chosen to be a Syrian, the best he could hope to achieve was to be a father of perhaps one Syrian tribe. Thankfully, he chose the vision of God – an international vision.
